audit_items.proof_trace: Block verification enforces max_blobs_per_block(epoch) runtime check (block_verification.rs:883-886), but data column gossip verification omits this check entirely—only verifying cells==commitments==proofs length consistency, not the epoch-specific upper bound.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker crafts data column sidecar with kzg_commitments.len() > max_blobs_per_block for the epoch (e.g., 9 commitments when epoch limit is 6). SSZ VariableList accepts up to MaxBlobCommitmentsPerBlock=4096. The data column passes gossip verification and propagates, causing resource exhaustion or consensus inconsistency when peers process oversized columns that would be rejected during block verification.
